report.title: `AnyPath` only matches one segment in URL walking
report.summary: `PathPattern::AnyPath` is documented and parsed as a catch-all path segment, but the URL walker treated it like a normal one-segment wildcard when more path segments remained.

impact: Routes such as `/files/<**path:rest>` matched `/files/a`, but failed to match deeper paths such as `/files/a/b/c`.

behavior_diff.expected: Once an `AnyPath` child is matched, traversal should stop and return that child, regardless of how many path segments remain.
behavior_diff.actual: The walker recursed into the `AnyPath` node when additional segments remained. Since the `AnyPath` node usually has no children, deeper paths returned `None`.

status.fix: Updated node-rooted and root-rooted walk implementations to recurse only when the matched child is not `AnyPath`.
